name: Derivative of sin(x)
question_text: What is the derivative of f(x) = sin(x) with respect to x?
general_feedback: The derivative of sine is cosine: d/dx [sin(x)] = cos(x). This follows from the small-angle approximation sin(h) ≈ h and the angle-addition identity applied in the limit definition of the derivative.
incorrect_feedback: Incorrect — recall the standard derivatives of the trigonometric functions.

answers.feedback: That is the derivative of cos(x), not sin(x).
That is the second derivative of sin(x), not the first.
tan(x) is sin(x) / cos(x); it is not the derivative of sin(x).
